While existing learning-to-hash models have many explicit hyperparameters to learn the optimal hash function, our work shows how to use adversarial learning's distribution matching trick to implicitly learn this optimal hash function without any hyperparameter. results.title: Remote Senior Data Scientist - Real Estate at Truelogic - Jobicy
highlights:
Remote Senior Data Scientist - Real Estate at Truelogic - Jobicy
...
Truelogic is seeking a Senior Data Scientist for a tech-forward real estate client. The role involves building predictive models, RAG pipelines, and knowledge graphs, while analyzing MLS data and designing product experiments. The ideal candidate has 5+ years of experience in data science, strong SQL and Python skills, and a proven track record of deploying LLM-powered workflows. This is a 100% remote position with competitive USD compensation and a focus on autonomy and results.
...
AI Insight The role requires advanced machine learning, RAG pipeline development, and production deployment, indicating a high level of technical complexity and problem-solving.
...
Data Science Machine Learning Predictive Modeling SQL Python RAG LLM A/B Testing Databricks Real Estate Analytics
...
I am thrilled to apply for the Senior Data Scientist position at your real estate brokerage. With over 5 years of experience in data science and machine learning, I have successfully built predictive models and RAG pipelines in production environments. My expertise in SQL, Python, and A/B testing aligns perfectly with your requirements.
...
I am particularly drawn to the opportunity to work with cutting-edge technologies like LLMs and knowledge graphs to drive actionable insights in real estate. I look forward to collaborating with your engineering and product teams to deliver data-driven solutions.
...
I would start by collecting historical lead data including interactions, property views, and conversion outcomes. I would engineer features from agent and listing attributes, then train a gradient boosting model. I would evaluate using AUC and precision-recall, and deploy via an API for real-time scoring.
...
I have built RAG pipelines for document Q&A systems. I used vector embeddings with a retrieval step using FAISS, then integrated with GPT-4 for generation. The pipeline was deployed on AWS Lambda with API Gateway, handling thousands of queries daily.
...
We’re looking for a data scientist to plug into our team and help us get more out of it, including building AI-enabled workflows on top of our data. You’d work alongside product, engineering, and brokerage ops.
...
Build and deploy advanced predictive models for lead scoring, agent matching, price and time-on-market estimation, and conversion likelihood.
...
Develop production-grade RAG and GraphRAG pipelines, construct complex knowledge graphs across listings, agents, and clients, and design retrieval and ranking systems for unstructured data.
...
Write robust data transforms in Databricks (using SQL and PySpark) and author highly performant SQL queries across ClickHouse and PostgreSQL.
...
Partner closely with engineering teams to ship model and LLM outputs to production environments, frequently utilizing Inngest.
...
Analyze MLS data to surface actionable market trends and directly feed data-driven product features.
...
Design and analyze product experiments utilizing PostHog, and build comprehensive dashboards and analyses within Hex and Metabase.
...
by translating ambiguous
...
5+ years of experience in data science or applied machine learning within a product-led company.
...
Solid hands-on Python expertise, specifically with libraries such as pandas, scikit-learn, and statsmodels.
...
Proven experience applying core ML concepts, including regression, classification, forecasting, and clustering, to real-world problems.
...
Demonstrated success building and deploying RAG or LLM-powered workflows in a live production environment, going beyond simple prototypes.
...
Strong background in A/B test design, execution, and statistical analysis.

results.title: Data Scientist - Multifamily Revenue Management | Zenvora
highlights:
Data Scientist - Multifamily Revenue Management | Zenvora
...
# Data Scientist - Multifamily Revenue Management
...
AI and data
...
products with triple-digit growth rates.
...
ApartmentIQ is the all-in-one data platform powering smarter decisions across multifamily—covering 40M units with daily property and market intelligence used for surveys, research, and revenue management. We're trusted by 65% of the NMHC Top 50. Our sister company, MavenAI, delivers AI-powered marketing automation built by industry experts and integrated directly with property management systems, serving over 2M units.
...
### The Role
...
We are seeking a Data Scientist to bridge the worlds of multifamily revenue management and data science. ApartmentIQ is the market intelligence layer that operators, asset managers, and RM platforms rely on to price millions of apartment units — and this role sits at the center of making that intelligence sharper, more actionable, and more defensible.
...
This is the right role for someone with real hands-on experience in how multifamily pricing decisions get made: the tradeoffs between occupancy and rate, how concessions affect net effective rent, how renewal strategy differs from new lease strategy, and how seasonality shapes leasing behavior. You'll apply that expertise to build data-driven products that help the industry price smarter.
...
You'll work closely with leadership, product, and engineering to design and deliver features that make multifamily pricing intelligence more actionable — and you'll be expected to use AI tools aggressively as a core part of how you work, not as an afterthought.
...
- Partner with
...
managers and engineers
...
define requirements and
...
ApartmentIQ's
...
management products.
...
- Translate revenue management workflows into scalable tools and features — you should be able to sit in a pricing call and immediately understand what the revenue manager is trying to solve.
...
- Apply LLMs and other AI techniques to accelerate research, automate workflows, and unlock new insights from structured and unstructured market data.
...
- Design and run experiments to measure whether our data and recommendations actually drive better RM outcomes for customers.
...
- Stay current on multifamily industry trends, data sources, AI/ML advances, and pricing methodologies.
...
- 3–5 years of experience in or alongside multifamily revenue management — as a pricing analyst, RM advisor, asset manager, or in a strategy or analytics role at a multifamily operator or tech company.
...
- Experience applying LLMs or AI/ML techniques to accelerate research, automate analysis, or extract insights from data.
...
- Strong quantitative and analytical skills with proficiency in SQL.
...
- Familiarity with modern data science practices, including model building, experimentation, and validation.
...
- Proficiency in Python or similar.
- Experience building customer-facing analytical products or dashboards.

results.title: [Remote] Yardi Lease Data Specialist | Joblume
highlights:
[Remote] Yardi Lease Data Specialist | Joblume
...
# [Remote] Yardi Lease Data Specialist
...
Posted Jun 29, 2026
...
Full-time

 Note: The job is a remote job and is open to candidates in USA. Realty Income Corporation aims to be a globally recognized leader in the S&P 100, committed to creating long-term value for all stakeholders. The Yardi Lease Data Specialist will manage database administration and lease administration tasks, ensuring accurate property and lease information is maintained in the Yardi database. Responsibilities Maintain Yardi Commercial Management database of record Accurately and timely input property and lease information for newly acquired properties Accurately and timely input all changes in property status due to changes, to include, but not limited to tenancy, developments and expansions, takings and condemnations Accurately and timely input all changes to tenant notice and billing information Create and maintain clear, concise records of audited checklists and notify internal parties upon completion, as necessary Keep database updated with current information, including, but not limited to updates to Notice addresses, property addresses, Tenant dba name and Tenant payment methods Ability to review and interpret lease provisions including, but not limited to Term, Parties under the Lease(s), and Rent figures Timely compile, verify, and provide primary review of property and lease information for new property acquisitions and accurately transfer data into Yardi Participate in Yardi testing as needed to include updated DAS templates and test databases related to company initiatives Run reports and review dashboards to ensure the data represented in the results.title: Machine Learning Engineer – Generative AI (LLM, RAG, Agents) – San Diego, California | Visa + Salary Insights
highlights:
Machine Learning Engineer – Generative AI (LLM, RAG, Agents) – San Diego, California | Visa + Salary Insights
...
# Machine Learning Engineer – Generative AI (LLM, RAG, Agents) – San Diego, California | Visa + Salary Insights
...
This role at :contentReference[oaicite:0]{index=0} focuses on building next-generation Generative AI systems for production-scale environments. The position is designed for engineers with strong expertise in Large Language Models (LLMs), Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), and agentic AI systems. The ideal candidate is someone who can move beyond experimentation into robust deployment, optimization, and evaluation of AI systems that integrate external knowledge sources. This is a high-impact engineering role within a core AI team, requiring both research fluency and production engineering discipline.
...
- 🌍 Visa Sponsorship: Likely available for strong candidates (not explicitly stated)
- ✈️ Relocation Support: Expected
...
San Diego engineering roles
...
- 🏠 Remote Type: Onsite / Hybrid
...
- ⏰ Timezone
...
🌐 Country Restrictions: Primarily US-based hiring
...
- 💰 Official Salary: $128,000 – $192,000
- 📊 Estimated Range: $140,000 – $210,000+ total compensation (with RSUs + bonus)
- 📈 Level: Mid–Senior Level (3+ years ML experience)
...
This role centers on building scalable Generative AI systems that combine LLMs, vector databases, and RAG pipelines into production-ready architectures. Engineers are expected to design systems that improve model accuracy by integrating external knowledge sources and optimizing retrieval pipelines. A major focus is developing agent-based workflows capable of autonomous reasoning and task execution across distributed environments.
...
Approximately 30% of the role involves designing and implementing RAG architectures, ensuring semantic retrieval quality and low-latency inference. Another 25% focuses on fine-tuning LLMs using domain-specific datasets to improve performance for targeted applications. Around 20% is dedicated to building evaluation frameworks that measure hallucination reduction, retrieval precision, and response quality.
...
The remaining time involves integrating PyTorch, TensorFlow, and modern ML infrastructure tools into scalable pipelines deployed on AWS/GCP/Azure. Engineers will also collaborate with cross-functional teams to deploy models using microservices and containerized environments. Strong emphasis is placed on production reliability, observability, and continuous model improvement.
...
- ✅ Must: Python programming at production level
- ✅ Must: Experience with LLMs and Generative AI
- ✅ Must: RAG architecture design and implementation
- ➕ Bonus: LangChain, LlamaIndex, Transformers
- ➕ Bonus: Reinforcement Learning experience
...
The engineer will design and deploy LLM-based systems integrated with external knowledge sources using RAG pipelines. Responsibilities include building scalable inference services, optimizing embedding models, and improving retrieval accuracy. A core part of the role involves fine-tuning large models for domain adaptation and ensuring stable performance under production constraints.
...
The role also requires building evaluation frameworks to assess hallucination rates, response relevance, and retrieval effectiveness. Engineers will implement agentic workflows capable of autonomous decision-making across multi-step tasks. Additionally, collaboration with infrastructure teams will ensure seamless deployment on AWS, GCP, or internal cloud systems using containerized microservices architectures.
...
RAG Systems: Implement retrieval-augmented generation pipelines using vector databases
...
LLM Fine-Tuning: Demonstrate fine-tuning large language models for domain adaptation
...
Agentic Workflows: Build autonomous AI agents using LangChain or similar frameworks
...
metrics for hallucination reduction and
...
Deploy scalable ML systems using PyTorch and cloud platforms

highlights:
Director, Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning | Wfh
...
# Director, Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning
...
### About the role:
...
Angi is seeking an exceptional Director, Artificial Intelligence & Machine Learning to be a driving force behind Angi's transformation. The role is to be a leader in our Artificial Intelligence team, tackling challenges such as integrating Angi's search with LLM Providers like OpenAI GPT and Google Gemini. This is a leadership role, leading a team of dedicated machine learning engineers to both develop ML prototypes and then take them to production. In addition to being a leader, we're also looking for someone who can dive into tech and get their hands dirty to solve some of Angi's biggest technical challenges.
...
- Leadership: Lead and grow a team of talented machine learning engineers, providing guidance, mentorship, and support to foster professional growth. For our most complex challenges, you will dive into the tech to work through solutioning alongside your team.
...
- LLM Deployment and Optimization: Oversee the fine-tuning and deployment of LLMs, including Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) in an agentic framework, with a focus on optimizing model performance and scalability to enhance user experience and drive business results.
...
- Integration with 3rd Party AI APIs: Oversee the integration and management of AI tools and third-party solutions, including OpenAI GPT, Google Gemini, Meta AI, and Salesforce AI, to enhance user experience and business performance.
...
- Engineering & Product Collaboration: Partner with engineering, product, and design teams to build robust, scalable real-time APIs, and work on the entire development process to seamlessly integrate AI solutions into our products.
...
- Innovation: Foster innovation within the team, exploring new approaches and techniques to solve complex business problems.
...
Who you are
...
- You have a Master's or Ph.D. in a computer science or quantitative field (e.g., Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, or related fields).
- You have 10+ years of experience in machine learning & engineering, with a focus on large-scale distributed application architecture for deploying ML models to production.
- You are an expert in machine learning deployment, API development, and real-time ML workloads.
- You are proficient in Python and comfortable diving deep into technical challenges alongside your team.
- You have excellent communication skills with the ability to convey complex technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders.

results.title: VP, Artificial Intelligence - Products & Services | CareerSync
highlights:
# VP, Artificial Intelligence - Products & Services
...
About the Role & TeamIDC is seeking a transformative AI leader to define and execute our enterprise-wide AI strategy—embedding cutting-edge artificial intelligence across both internal operations and customer-facing products. This is a rare opportunity to shape how a global data and intelligence company harnesses AI, from agentic workflows and large language models to predictive analytics and AI-native product experiences. Reporting directly to the CTO, the VP of AI will lead a high-performing technical team, partner across business units, and drive measurable business impact through intelligent, responsible, and scalable AI solutions. The ideal candidate combines deep technical expertise in modern AI—including generative AI, LLMs, and agentic systems—with the strategic acumen to align AI investments to revenue growth and competitive differentiation. IDC sits at the intersection of proprietary data, expert human insight, and global technology reach—an ideal foundation for AI transformation. Key opportunity areas include: AI-augmented research & analyst productivity — deploying LLM-powered tools to accelerate data synthesis, report generation, and market forecasting. Intelligent data products — embedding predictive models and AI-driven insights directly into IDC's subscription and advisory offerings. Agentic content & marketing workflows — leveraging generative AI to personalize content, automate content pipelines, and optimize Foundry's MarTech capabilities. Enterprise AI infrastructure — building shared platforms, APIs, and MLOps practices that enable AI adoption at scale across all IDC business units. What You’ll Do
...
AI Strategy & Vision: Partner with the CEO, CTO, CIO, and business unit heads to define IDC's multi-year AI roadmap—spanning generative AI, agentic systems, predictive analytics, and AI-native product development. Technical Leadership: Architect and oversee IDC's AI platform and infrastructure, ensuring scalability, security, and alignment with enterprise data strategy. Product Delivery: Develop and execute IDC’s approach to agentic AI technology, thereby delivering the centerpiece of IDC Quanta, our cross-channel technology intelligence layer. Team Building: Recruit, develop, and retain a world-class AI team spanning ML engineers, data scientists, AI product managers, and MLOps specialists. Stakeholder Alignment: Translate complex AI capabilities into clear business value for executives and department heads; maintain transparent goals and resource planning. Ecosystem & Partnerships: Evaluate and manage AI technology partnerships, vendor relationships, and open-source contributions to accelerate IDC's AI capabilities. What You Bring
...
PhD. or Master's degree in Computer Science, AI/ML, Statistics, or a related quantitative field (or equivalent experience).
...
7+ years of experience in applied machine learning, AI engineering, or data science—with at least 3 years in senior technical leadership roles.
...
products and platforms that generated measurable business impact (revenue growth, cost reduction, or product differentiation).
...
Deep expertise in modern AI/ML and agentic/generative systems design.
...
Hands-on fluency with generative AI technologies: LLM fine-tuning, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), prompt engineering, and agentic frameworks (e.g., LangChain, LlamaIndex, AutoGen).
...
Strong engineering foundation: Python, SQL, cloud ML platforms (AWS SageMaker, GCP Vertex AI, or Azure ML), and modern MLOps practices (CI/CD, model monitoring, feature stores).
...
Familiarity with knowledge graphs, semantic search, or AI applications in content and market research workflows.
...
Experience with responsible AI frameworks, model governance, and AI regulatory compliance (EU AI Act, NIST AI RMF).
...
Track record of building AI developer platforms or shared ML infrastructure used by multiple product teams.
